text: Simon Petrus, servus et apostolus Jesu Christi, iis qui coæqualem nobiscum sortiti sunt fidem in justitia Dei nostri, et Salvatoris Jesu Christi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Gratia vobis, et pax adimpleatur in cognitione Dei, et Christi Jesu Domini nostri :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quomodo omnia nobis divinæ virtutis suæ, quæ ad vitam et pietatem donata sunt, per cognitionem ejus, qui vocavit nos propria gloria, et virtute,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: per quem maxima, et pretiosa nobis promissa donavit : ut per hæc efficiamini divinæ consortes naturæ : fugientes ejus, quæ in mundo est, concupiscentiæ corruptionem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vos autem curam omnem subinferentes, ministrate in fide vestra virtutem, in virtute autem scientiam,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: in scientia autem abstinentiam, in abstinentia autem patientiam, in patientia autem pietatem,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: in pietate autem amorem fraternitatis, in amore autem fraternitatis caritatem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc enim si vobiscum adsint, et superent, non vacuos nec sine fructu vos constituent in Domini nostri Jesu Christi cognitione.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cui enim non præsto sunt hæc, cæcus est, et manu tentans, oblivionem accipiens purgationis veterum suorum delictorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quapropter fratres, magis satagite ut per bona opera certam vestram vocationem, et electionem faciatis : hæc enim facientes, non peccabitis aliquando.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sic enim abundanter ministrabitur vobis introitus in æternum regnum Domini nostri et Salvatoris Jesu Christi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propter quod incipiam vos semper commonere de his : et quidem scientes et confirmatos vos in præsenti veritate.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Justum autem arbitror quamdiu sum in hoc tabernaculo, suscitare vos in commonitione :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: certus quod velox est depositio tabernaculi mei secundum quod et Dominus noster Jesus Christus significavit mihi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dabo autem operam et frequenter habere vos post obitum meum, ut horum memoriam faciatis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non enim doctas fabulas secuti notam fecimus vobis Domini nostri Jesu Christi virtutem et præsentiam : sed speculatores facti illius magnitudinis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Accipiens enim a Deo Patre honorem et gloriam, voce delapsa ad eum hujuscemodi a magnifica gloria : Hic est Filius meus dilectus, in quo mihi complacui, ipsum audite.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et hanc vocem nos audivimus de cælo allatam, cum essemus cum ipso in monte sancto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et habemus firmiorem propheticum sermonem : cui benefacitis attendentes quasi lucernæ lucenti in caliginoso loco donec dies elucescat, et lucifer oriatur in cordibus vestris :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: hoc primum intelligentes quod omnis prophetia Scripturæ propria interpretatione non fit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non enim voluntate humana allata est aliquando prophetia : sed Spiritu Sancto inspirati, locuti sunt sancti Dei homines.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fuerunt vero et pseudoprophetæ in populo, sicut et in vobis erunt magistri mendaces, qui introducent sectas perditionis : et eum qui emit eos, Dominum negant, superducentes sibi celerem perditionem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et multi sequentur eorum luxurias, per quos via veritatis blasphemabitur :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et in avaritia fictis verbis de vobis negotiabuntur : quibus judicium jam olim non cessat : et perditio eorum non dormitat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si enim Deus angelis peccantibus non pepercit, sed rudentibus inferni detractos in tartarum tradidit cruciandos, in judicium reservari.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et originali mundo non pepercit, sed octavum Noë justitiæ præconem custodivit, diluvium mundo impiorum inducens.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et civitates Sodomorum et Gomorrhæorum in cinerem redigens, eversione damnavit : exemplum eorum, qui impie acturi sunt, ponens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et justum Lot oppressum a nefandorum injuria, ac luxuriosa conversatione eripuit :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: aspectu enim, et auditu justus erat : habitans apud eos, qui de die in diem animam justam iniquis operibus cruciabant.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Novit Dominus pios de tentatione eripere : iniquos vero in diem judicii reservare cruciandos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Magis autem eos, qui post carnem in concupiscentia immunditiæ ambulant, dominationemque contemnunt, audaces, sibi placentes, sectas non metuunt introducere blasphemantes :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ubi angeli fortitudine, et virtute cum sint majores, non portant adversum se execrabile judicium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hi vero velut irrationabilia pecora, naturaliter in captionem, et in perniciem in his quæ ignorant blasphemantes in corruptione sua peribunt,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: percipientes mercedem injustitiæ, voluptatem existimantes diei delicias : coinquinationes, et maculæ deliciis affluentes, in conviviis suis luxuriantes vobiscum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: oculos habentes plenos adulterii, et incessabilis delicti. Pellicientes animas instabiles, cor exercitatum avaritia habentes, maledictionis filii :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: derelinquentes rectam viam erraverunt, secuti viam Balaam ex Bosor, qui mercedem iniquitatis amavit :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: correptionem vero habuit suæ vesaniæ : subjugale mutum animal, hominis voce loquens, prohibuit prophetæ insipientiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hi sunt fontes sine aqua, et nebulæ turbinibus exagitatæ, quibus caligo tenebrarum reservatur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Superba enim vanitatis loquentes, pelliciunt in desideriis carnis luxuriæ eos, qui paululum effugiunt, qui in errore conversantur :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: libertatem illis promittentes, cum ipsi servi sint corruptionem : a quo enim quis superatus est, hujus et servus est.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si enim refugientes coinquinationes mundi in cognitione Domini nostri, et Salvatoris Jesu Christi, his rursus implicati superantur : facta sunt eis posteriora deteriora prioribus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Melius enim erat illis non cognoscere viam justitiæ, quam post agnitionem, retrorsum converti ab eo, quod illis traditum est, sancto mandato.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Contigit enim eis illud veri proverbii : Canis reversus ad suum vomitum : et, Sus lota in volutabro luti.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hanc ecce vobis, carissimi, secundam scribo epistolam, in quibus vestram excito in commonitione sinceram mentem :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut memores sitis eorum, quæ prædixi, verborum, a sanctis prophetis et apostolorum vestrorum, præceptorum Domini et Salvatoris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hoc primum scientes, quod venient in novissimis diebus in deceptione illusores, juxta proprias concupiscentias ambulantes,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: dicentes : Ubi est promissio, aut adventus ejus ? ex quo enim patres dormierunt, omnia sic perseverant ab initio creaturæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Latet enim eos hoc volentes, quod cæli erant prius, et terra de aqua, et per aquam consistens Dei verbo :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: per quæ, ille tunc mundus aqua inundatus, periit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cæli autem, qui nunc sunt, et terra eodem verbo repositi sunt, igni reservati in diem judicii, et perditionis impiorum hominum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Unum vero hoc non lateat vos, carissimi, quia unus dies apud Dominum sicut mille anni, et mille anni sicut dies unus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non tardat Dominus promissionem suam, sicut quidam existimant : sed patienter agit propter vos, nolens aliquos perire, sed omnes ad pœnitentiam reverti.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Adveniet autem dies Domini ut fur : in quo cæli magno impetu transient, elementa vero calore solventur, terra autem et quæ in ipsa sunt opera, exurentur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum igitur hæc omnia dissolvenda sunt, quales oportet vos esse in sanctis conversationibus, et pietatibus,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: exspectantes, et properantes in adventum diei Domini, per quem cæli ardentes solventur, et elementa ignis ardore tabescent ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Novos vero cælos, et novam terram secundum promissa ipsius exspectamus, in quibus justitia habitat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propter quod, carissimi, hæc exspectantes, satagite immaculati, et inviolati ei inveniri in pace :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et Domini nostri longanimitatem, salutem arbitremini : sicut et carissimus frater noster Paulus secundum datam sibi sapientiam scripsit vobis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sicut et omnibus epistolis, loquens in eis de his in quibus sunt quædam difficilia intellectu, quæ indocti et instabiles depravant, sicut et ceteras Scripturas, ad suam ipsorum perditionem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vos igitur fratres, præscientes custodite, ne insipientium errore traducti excidatis a propria firmitate :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: crescite vero in gratia, et in cognitione Domini nostri, et Salvatoris Jesu Christi. Ipsi gloria et nunc, et in diem æternitatis. Amen.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)
